personal.name: Akash Sharma
personal.title: Software Developer & AI Systems Engineer
personal.tagline: Architecting tomorrow's infrastructure with today's intelligence.

bio.short_bio: Building production-grade backend systems where AI meets enterprise reliability. Three years of crafting platforms that process millions of transactions, detect fraud in milliseconds, and turn complex data into intelligent decisions.
long_bio: I'm a full-stack software developer who specializes in the systems most engineers avoid — the ones that need to be fast, reliable, and intelligent simultaneously. My work lives at the intersection of backend infrastructure, data engineering, and applied AI.

Currently completing my MSc in Artificial Intelligence for Business at National College of Ireland, I bridge academic rigor with production realities. I've architected banking platforms with real-time fraud detection, built RAG systems over enterprise documents, and engineered ETL pipelines that turned days of manual work into automated minutes.
Based in Dublin, open to global opportunities. I'm drawn to teams building systems that matter — where complexity is a feature, not a bug, and where the work has measurable impact on real users.

projects.name: ESOTERIC Bank Intelligence Platform
projects.tagline: Enterprise banking infrastructure with embedded AI governance
story_paragraphs: Traditional banking systems struggle to combine real-time transaction processing with the compliance and observability that regulators demand. ESOTERIC is my answer — an end-to-end platform that handles everything from synthetic data generation to executive AI copilots.

I architected a complete data pipeline: Python ETL feeding a PostgreSQL star-schema warehouse, with automated quality gates that fail the pipeline on integrity errors. The system includes fraud detection engines, AML compliance with SAR triggering, and a governance layer that tracks regulatory drift.

What sets this apart is the observability stack — OpenTelemetry traces every transaction, Prometheus collects custom business metrics, and Grafana dashboards surface anomalies in real-time. I stress-tested it with multi-vector crisis simulations: coordinated fraud, liquidity deterioration, AML smurfing patterns. All defenses held.
This project taught me that production systems aren't about clever code — they're about systems that fail loudly, recover gracefully, and tell you the truth about their state.

projects.name: EAIKA — Enterprise AI Knowledge Assistant
projects.tagline: Private enterprise RAG application with semantic intelligence
story_paragraphs: Organizations sit on mountains of internal knowledge — wikis, PDFs, contracts, technical docs — that no one can effectively search. EAIKA solves this by combining vector embeddings with LLM reasoning.

The architecture is modular by design: FastAPI handles document ingestion and chunking, OpenAI generates embeddings, ChromaDB stores and retrieves by semantic similarity, and GPT-4o-mini synthesizes context-aware answers. Streamlit provides the interface for upload, search, and conversation.

I focused heavily on the RAG pipeline quality — chunking strategy, embedding choice, retrieval ranking, and prompt engineering all impact answer accuracy more than the LLM itself. The result is a system that doesn't just search documents; it understands them.
Building this clarified for me that the future of enterprise software isn't 'AI-powered features' — it's reimagining workflows around what LLMs make possible.

projects.name: Automated Data Intelligence Pipeline
projects.tagline: Self-healing ETL infrastructure with embedded quality governance
story_paragraphs: Operations and finance teams were drowning in manual reporting — Excel sheets, copy-paste workflows, daily reconciliation. I rebuilt the data infrastructure from the ground up.

I designed and deployed 8+ Python-based ETL pipelines feeding a centralized PostgreSQL warehouse. The critical innovation was the validation framework — automated reconciliation, quality gates, and self-healing retry logic that kept the pipelines running even when source systems hiccupped.

The result reshaped how the company operated. Executive reports that took days now generated in hours. Data errors that previously made it to dashboards were caught before they could mislead decisions. Five business stakeholders went from data consumers to confident decision-makers.
This was where I learned that data engineering isn't about moving data — it's about building trust between people and the numbers they make decisions on.

projects.name: ML-Powered Customer Intelligence
projects.tagline: Production machine learning for sales optimization
story_paragraphs: Sales teams spent equal time on hot leads and cold ones. The opportunity was obvious — predict which prospects were actually likely to convert, before reps invested hours.

I analyzed datasets of 100K+ customer records to engineer features that captured behavioral intent — engagement patterns, demographic signals, interaction histories. The lead scoring model used supervised learning to rank prospects, while customer segmentation clustered users into actionable archetypes.

The output integrated directly into the sales workflow — reps saw priority scores, segment context, and recommended actions. Conversion rates improved measurably as the team focused effort where it mattered most.

This project was my introduction to the reality of production ML — that the hardest part isn't training models, it's the feature engineering, data quality, and integration with how humans actually work.

projects.name: AI Agent PR Review Bot
projects.tagline: Autonomous GitHub reviewing microservice with safety hook guardrails
story_paragraphs: Developing microservices that orchestrate LLM agents requires solid safety triggers, dynamic function mappings, and fast async structures to prevent gateway timeout drops.

I implemented a Spring Boot service listening to GitHub webhook triggers. It parses pull request events and calls an agent orchestrated via Spring AI. The agent maps tool schemas dynamically (static code analysis, file retrieval) and comments feedback back onto the pull request.

Safety is enforced via custom OnToolErrorHook controls and validation layers that ensure agent constraints remain immutable. It executes asynchronously using CompletableFuture to respond within webhook SLA windows.
This prototype demonstrates modern microservice agent design, incorporating observability logs and micro-metrics alongside clean Spring architectures.
